title: Who Will Win the 2026 World Cup?
text:
The Tournament
The 2026 FIFA World Cup is being hosted across the United States, Canada, and Mexico, making it the first ever 48-team World Cup.
More teams. More games. More unpredictability.
The question on everyone’s mind: who’s lifting the trophy?

title: Who Will Win the 2026 World Cup?
text:
The Contenders
Based on historical performance, FIFA rankings, squad depth, and recent form:



Rank
Team
Key Strength
Titles




1
Argentina
Defending champions, winning mentality
3

Most titles ever, home continent advantage
5

2

Euro 2024 champions, young and hungry
1


5
England
Strong Premier League core, consistently improving
1



The data points to South America. But football is never that simple.

title: Who Will Win the 2026 World Cup?
text:
My Prediction: Argentina
After running classification models on historical match data, team rankings, and player statistics:

Defending champions with proven tournament DNA
Messi’s final World Cup creates unmatched motivation
Best squad chemistry of any national team right now
Massive fanbase on American soil

Vamos Argentina!

title: Beyond the Spreadsheet
text: I grew up in Cairo, a city of 20 million people where the energy never stops and the street food is unmatched. Today I call San Diego home, where I’m finishing my master’s in business analytics at UCSD. Between those two cities, I’ve picked up a few hobbies, a lot of stamps in my passport, and an opinion about every coffee shop I’ve ever walked into.

title: Beyond the Spreadsheet
text:
Passport Stamps
I gravitate toward places with history, good food, and interesting streets to get lost in. Italy keeps pulling me back. I’ve wandered through Florence, soaked up the sun on the Italian Riviera, stood on the cliffs of Cinque Terre, and had what I’m fairly sure was a life-changing cappuccino next to the Colosseum.






Florence







Cinque Terre







Coffee in Rome







Prague at golden hour





I’ve also skied the Austrian Alps, fed ducks in Hyde Park, explored charming old towns after dark, and wandered through misty green valleys with nothing but an umbrella and nowhere to be.






Skiing in Austria







Hyde Park, London







Old town Austria







Misty Austrian countryside

title: Beyond the Spreadsheet
text:
Football is Life
I don’t just watch football. I travel for it. Catching a match at Camp Nou or the Metropolitano is the kind of experience that stays with you. The roar of 90,000 people, the floodlights, the scarves in the air. If there’s a World Cup or Champions League match happening, I’m either there or glued to a screen.






Camp Nou, Barcelona







Metropolitano, Madrid

title: Beyond the Spreadsheet
text:
On Two Wheels
San Diego has some of the best cycling routes in the country and I take full advantage. I ride with a local group most weekends. Early mornings, coastal roads, ocean views. It’s the best way to start a day and the closest thing I have to meditation.

title: Beyond the Spreadsheet
text:
What’s Cooking
The kitchen is my other creative outlet. What started as a pandemic experiment has turned into a real passion. I make my own pizza dough, bake sourdough from scratch, and have a baked brie recipe that has never failed to impress.






Pizza night







Sourdough







Pizza ring







Baked brie

Cairo, Egypt
